title: livespec implementation-gap report
description: Machine-readable gap report between SPECIFICATION/ and the actual livespec repository. Authoritative shape comes from SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md §Contracts §'Implementation-gap report shape'. Overwritten on every run of the refresh-gaps skill (or its just wrapper). Resolved gaps disappear from this file once `implement` verifies their closure; closed beads issues retain their gap-id labels for audit but no longer appear here.

schema_version.description: Bumped only when the report shape changes incompatibly.

generated_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp at which refresh-gaps produced this report.

spec_sources.description: Git blob hashes for the SPECIFICATION/ files read at generation time. The implement skill rejects the report as stale if any hash no longer matches the current file on disk.

inspection.description: What refresh-gaps inspected and what it deliberately or unavoidably skipped. Findings backed by concrete file/command evidence are stronger than findings inferred from absence.

scopes_skipped.description: Sub-trees deliberately not walked, with one-line reason each.

run_id.description: UUID assigned to this refresh-gaps invocation. Quoted in beads close --note as the verification run id (per non-functional-requirements.md §Constraints close-with-audit-fields rule).

summary.description: Aggregate counts. Each sub-object's keys are open-ended (the value-set lives in the corresponding gap field's enum); 'unassigned' or omitted bands are tolerated.

git_blob_sha.description: Git blob hash (sha1) as reported by `git hash-object <file>`.

id.description: Stable identifier. Once assigned to an entry, it MUST persist across regenerations as long as the underlying gap remains; do not renumber on re-run.

area.description: Which sub-system the gap belongs to. `spec` = SPECIFICATION/ tree itself; `plugin` = .claude-plugin/ shipped surface; `implementation` = .claude/plugins/livespec-implementation/ + dev-tooling/implementation/; `dev-tooling` = the enforcement-suite checks; `tests` = pytest tree; `config` = top-level toolchain (.mise.toml, justfile, lefthook.yml, pyproject.toml); `ci` = .github/workflows; `docs` = README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, etc.

severity.description: Author-assigned severity. blocking = the workflow does not run at all without this; missing = required artifact entirely absent; partial = artifact exists but incomplete; drift = artifact exists but diverged from spec; cosmetic = wording / naming nit.

priority.description: Operator-assigned priority band. Optional; omit on new gaps until classified. The plan skill sorts by priority before severity. P0 = critical / unblock-immediately; P1 = high; P2 = normal (default when classifying); P3 = backlog.

spec_refs.description: Anchor links of the form `<file>.md#<slug>` (e.g. `non-functional-requirements.md#beads-invariants`) that the gap is claimed against.

observed.description: What the repository actually exhibits, or 'not present' / 'not invocable' / etc. when the artifact is wholly absent.

evidence.description: Concrete pointers — file paths, command outputs, git blob hashes — that support the finding.

evidence_kind.description: Kind of evidence the entry rests on. `absent` = the absence-of-X is itself the evidence (e.g. file does not exist).

destructive_to_fix.description: True when any plausible fix involves dropping data, rewriting published commits, force-pushing, or other hard-to-reverse actions. The implement skill hard-stops on this flag and surfaces to the user before proceeding.

destructive_reason.description: Short phrase naming the destructive risk. MUST be non-null when destructive_to_fix is true; null otherwise.

fix_hint.description: One-sentence nudge for the implement skill — not a full plan.

depends_on.description: Gap ids that MUST be resolved before this one. Use only for genuinely forced orderings (e.g. a Python script depends on its schema being authored).
